QF.id: Taxation of Debt and Equity Products_10

question.text: Section 94(8) on bonus stripping applies when an investor buys units of a mutual fund within _______ before the record date, receives bonus units, and sells the original units within _______ after the record date at a loss.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 94(8) is the anti-avoidance provision against bonus stripping. It applies when: (1) units are purchased within 3 months BEFORE the record date for bonus, (2) bonus units are allotted, and (3) the original units are sold within 9 months AFTER the record date at a loss. In such cases, the loss to the extent of the per-unit NAV of bonus units is disallowed and added to the cost of the bonus units. **The Trap:** The asymmetric time periods (3 months before, 9 months after) are easy to confuse. Option D (3 months/3 months) seems logical as a symmetric rule but is wrong. The 9-month post-record-date window is deliberately wider to prevent investors from simply waiting a few months to sell. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A tests bonus stripping provisions in the context of mutual fund distributor responsibilities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember "3 before, 9 after" — together they span a 12-month window around the record date. If a client is planning to buy units near a bonus record date, warn them about the Section 94(8) implications on potential losses.
